findings.title: localStorage.getItem at module evaluation throws SecurityError in Safari private browsing, crashing app init
findings.why_it_matters: Line 196 executes `localStorage.getItem(SHOW_CLOSED_KEY)` at module evaluation time (outside any function, directly in the `<script setup>` body). In Safari private browsing, `localStorage` access throws a `SecurityError` synchronously. Because this is not inside a try/catch, the entire module fails to initialise, the app never mounts, and the user sees a blank screen. This is a self-hosted app; iOS Safari private browsing is a plausible runtime environment.
evidence: App.vue line 196: `const _stored = localStorage.getItem(SHOW_CLOSED_KEY)` — bare call, no try/catch, at module-level script setup evaluation
App.vue line 210: `localStorage.setItem(SHOW_CLOSED_KEY, String(showClosed.value))` — also bare, will throw on the same toggle action
App.vue line 323: `localStorage.getItem(COMPACT_KEY)` — same pattern for compact header; pre-existing but same class of bug
App.vue line 228: `localStorage.setItem(COMPACT_KEY, ...)` — same pattern for compact header write
Safari private browsing throws SecurityError on any localStorage access per the HTML spec (storage quota = 0, throws on getItem too in some versions)

findings.title: handleStatusChange does not catch updateStatus failure — drag-and-drop silently corrupts UI state on API error
findings.why_it_matters: When the user drags a card to a new column, `handleStatusChange` calls `await updateStatus(id, status)` with no try/catch. If the PATCH fails (network blip, 401 token expiry, 500 from server), the promise rejects and propagates as an unhandled rejection. Meanwhile vuedraggable has already moved the card in the local `columns` reactive state inside KanbanBoard. The watch on `props.applications` only fires when the parent reloads data, but `refreshApplication` is never called because the await threw. The card stays in the wrong column visually while the server has the old status. The user has no feedback that anything went wrong.
evidence: App.vue lines 293-312: `handleStatusChange` — `await updateStatus(id, status)` at line 295, no surrounding try/catch
App.vue line 295: if updateStatus throws, lines 296-311 never execute — no toast, no rollback, no refresh
KanbanBoard.vue lines 269-276: `onChange` emits `status-change` after vuedraggable has already mutated `columns[status]` locally
KanbanBoard.vue lines 253-261: columns are rebuilt only when `props.applications` changes; a failed API call means the parent never reloads, so the column stays in the mutated state
No global axios error interceptor in api.js that would surface the error

findings.title: Undo action in toast also lacks error handling — second API call failure is an unhandled rejection
findings.why_it_matters: The undo action callback at lines 305-309 calls `await updateStatus(id, prevStatus)` inside an async lambda with no try/catch. If the undo PATCH fails, the promise rejection escapes the toast system. The user clicked Undo, got no response, and the application is now in an indeterminate state that requires a page reload to recover.
evidence: App.vue lines 304-310: `action: async () => { await updateStatus(id, prevStatus); logoTrigger.value++; await refreshApplication(id) }` — no try/catch
useToast composable is not shown but undo callbacks are typically fire-and-forget; the rejection would be unhandled

findings.title: scrollIntoView race: closedGroupRef may be null when showClosed watcher fires on mobile
findings.why_it_matters: The watcher on `props.showClosed` at line 304 calls `closedGroupRef.value?.scrollIntoView(...)` after `nextTick`. The ref is bound via `bindClosedRef` to the closed group `<div>` which is rendered with `v-if="showClosed"`. Vue's `v-if` insertion and the ref callback run during the same flush as the watcher's `nextTick`. In practice the optional chaining (`?.`) masks a null ref silently — the scroll just does nothing. The user taps 'Show X closed' on mobile and the closed panel appears but doesn't scroll into view, requiring a manual scroll to find the new content. This is not a crash but a silent UX failure.
evidence: KanbanBoard.vue lines 304-309: watcher fires when `props.showClosed` becomes true, awaits nextTick, then calls `closedGroupRef.value?.scrollIntoView`
KanbanBoard.vue line 132: the closed group div is rendered with `v-if="showClosed"` — it is not in the DOM when showClosed is false
KanbanBoard.vue lines 296-298: `bindClosedRef` is the template ref callback; it is only called once the v-if condition renders the element
nextTick guarantees the DOM is updated but the timing of `bindClosedRef` call relative to the watcher callback execution needs verification

findings.title: onClosedChange emits status-change for dragged-in card without checking for in-flight status change
findings.why_it_matters: When a card is dragged from an active column into the closed drop zone, `onClosedChange` fires with `evt.added`. The code emits `status-change` to set the status to 'rejected' (line 290). However, the closed draggable has `put: false` on its group config (line 63), which means vuedraggable should prevent drops into it — but the drop target accepts items without validation of their current status against double-emission. If a rapid drag triggers both `onChange` (from the source column's `removed` event) and `onClosedChange` (from the target's `added` event), two `status-change` emissions can fire in quick succession for the same card. The second PATCH would race with the first.
evidence: KanbanBoard.vue line 63: `:group="{ name: 'applications', pull: true, put: false }"` — `put: false` means cards cannot be dropped INTO the closed zone from outside; this means `evt.added` in `onClosedChange` should never fire for external drags
KanbanBoard.vue lines 287-293: the `evt.added` branch in `onClosedChange` emits status-change to 'rejected' — this path is only reachable if put:false is somehow bypassed
Requires verification: confirm whether vuedraggable `put: false` fully prevents `added` events from firing for external group drags

findings.title: TimelineView: segmentStyle passes `isTerminalStage` based only on app.status === 'rejected', ignoring 'accepted'
findings.why_it_matters: The template calls `segmentStyle(seg, app.status === 'rejected')` at line 61. This means the grayscale/brightness filter (lines 161-163) is only applied to rejected applications, not accepted ones. The intent stated in the review context is to visually quiet 'rejected' cards/segments. This is intentional per the design spec ('accepted' = success = full colour). However, if the intent changes, the hardcoded equality check is fragile. This is a minor design consistency note, not a reliability issue.
evidence: TimelineView.vue line 61: `segmentStyle(seg, app.status === 'rejected')` — only rejected gets the muted filter
TimelineView.vue lines 51-52: label classes use `app.status === 'rejected'` for quieting, consistent with the segment style approach
This is aligned with design intent (accepted = positive outcome = full colour) but differs from isTerminal() which covers both

residual_risks: The `loadApplications` function in App.vue (line 232) has no error handling — if fetchApplications throws on initial load, `applications.value` stays as an empty array and the user sees an empty board with no error message. This is pre-existing.
The `onMounted` handler (line 320) calls `loadApplications()` without await and without catch — a load failure during mount produces an unhandled rejection. Pre-existing.
The `closedPartition.js` utility is imported in KanbanBoard but not reviewed here — any edge cases in partition logic could silently mis-categorize cards.
testing_gaps: No test covers the Safari private browsing localStorage failure path — the E2E tests run in Chromium/Firefox where localStorage works.
No test verifies that a failed PATCH in handleStatusChange results in a toast and re-sync — the drag interaction in E2E tests does not simulate network errors.
The scrollIntoView behaviour on mobile (closedGroupRef race) has no automated assertion — the E2E test only checks visibility, not scroll position.
